title: Does background music really increase revenue? — Daniel Fox
meta_description: The effect sizes are large: roughly 15% more per check from turning the volume down, 40% more bar revenue from slower tempo, $4,600 a day in a supermarket, 12% from tempo and key together. What the revenue research shows, and the conditions behind it.

schema.headline: Does background music really increase revenue?
schema.description: What field experiments on real transactions show about background music and revenue: volume, tempo, musical key, and the crowding condition that flips the answer.
